writers-room-evaluate.name: Writers Room — Prose Evaluation
writers-room-evaluate.description: Editorial pass on a Writers Room draft: logline, summary, themes, strengths, issues with excerpts, and concrete suggestions.

writers-room-format.name: Writers Room — Auto-format Prose
writers-room-format.description: Copy-edit pass on a Writers Room draft: paragraphing, dialogue formatting, whitespace, punctuation, and consistent chapter/scene markdown headings. Returns cleaned prose only.

manuscript-reformat.name: Manuscript — Reformat (fix paste artifacts)
manuscript-reformat.description: Repair PDF/paste formatting artifacts in a manuscript section — hard-wrapped paragraphs, split drop-caps, hyphen-broken words, and orphaned/duplicated quotation marks — WITHOUT changing any words. Returns the reformatted text only; an integrity guard rejects the result if any letters/digits changed.

writers-room-script.name: Writers Room — Adapt to Scene Script
writers-room-script.description: Adapt a Writers Room prose draft into a scene-by-scene screenplay-style breakdown with sluglines, action, dialogue, and per-scene visual prompts the image-gen pipeline can render.

writers-room-characters.name: Writers Room — Character Profiles
writers-room-characters.description: Extract a character bible from a Writers Room draft: name, aliases, role, image-gen-ready physical description, personality, and background. Preserves user edits and flags fields the prose does not yet support so the writer knows what to add.

writers-room-places.name: Writers Room — Setting / World Bible
writers-room-places.description: Extract a setting/world bible from a Writers Room draft: per-location descriptions keyed by screenplay slugline (palette, era, weather, recurring details). Storyboard scenes match their slugline back to a setting and inject its description into the image prompt for visual continuity.

writers-room-objects.name: Writers Room — Recurring Objects
writers-room-objects.description: Extract a recurring-objects bible from a Writers Room draft: symbolic or physically significant items that appear across multiple scenes (the letter, the fedora, a keepsake). Captures description and narrative significance so the writer can validate continuity.

writers-room-continue.name: Writers Room — Live Continuation
writers-room-continue.description: Live Creative Director feedback: from the prose around the cursor, propose 2–4 short optional continuation beats, prose snippets, or dialogue lines the writer can accept. Drives the opt-in live-mode side panel in the editor.

writers-room-cd-bridge.name: Writers Room — Creative Director Bridge
writers-room-cd-bridge.description: From the prose around the cursor, propose a short Creative Director treatment — a logline, synopsis, overall visual treatment (styleSpec), and 2–6 filmable scenes (intent + visual prompt + duration). The writer reviews it and sends it into a new Creative Director project. Drives the opt-in live-mode side panel in the editor.

catalog-ideas-scenes-concepts.name: Catalog Ingest — Ideas / Scenes / Concepts
catalog-ideas-scenes-concepts.description: Extract reusable narrative fragments — ideas (premises), scenes (dramatic beats), and concepts (lore, magic systems, factions) — from a raw user-pasted scrap into the Creative Ingredients Catalog. Runs alongside the character/place/object writers-room passes during catalog ingest.

cd-treatment.name: Creative Director — Treatment
cd-treatment.description: Plan a long-form generated-video project: write the logline, synopsis, and scene-by-scene treatment.

cd-evaluate.name: Creative Director — Scene Evaluation
cd-evaluate.description: Evaluate a freshly-rendered scene against the project style spec and scene intent. Reads multi-frame samples to judge intent across the full clip timeline.

app-detection.name: App Detection
app-detection.description: Analyze a project directory and detect its configuration

cos-agent-briefing.name: CoS Agent Briefing
cos-agent-briefing.description: Brief a sub-agent on their assigned task

cos-evaluate.name: CoS Task Evaluation
cos-evaluate.description: Evaluate pending tasks and decide what to work on

cos-self-improvement.name: CoS Self Improvement
cos-self-improvement.description: Analyze system performance and suggest improvements

memory-evaluate.name: Memory Evaluation
memory-evaluate.description: Evaluate agent output to extract useful memories

brain-weekly-review.name: Brain Weekly Review
brain-weekly-review.description: Generate weekly review and open loops analysis

soul-contradiction-detector.name: Soul Contradiction Detector
soul-contradiction-detector.description: Analyze soul documents for internal contradictions and inconsistencies

soul-enrichment.name: Soul Enrichment
soul-enrichment.description: Generate follow-up questions to enrich soul documents

soul-enrichment-process.name: Soul Enrichment Processor
soul-enrichment-process.description: Process enrichment answers for inclusion in soul documents

soul-test-generator.name: Soul Test Generator
soul-test-generator.description: Generate behavioral tests from soul content

soul-test-scorer.name: Soul Test Scorer
soul-test-scorer.description: Score AI responses against expected soul behavioral patterns

values-alignment-scorer.name: Values-Alignment Scorer
values-alignment-scorer.description: Grade whether a twin response is consistent with the user's ranked values hierarchy

adversarial-boundary-scorer.name: Adversarial Boundary Scorer
adversarial-boundary-scorer.description: Grade whether a twin held or breached a stated boundary under an adversarial manipulation attempt

multi-turn-consistency-scorer.name: Multi-Turn Consistency Scorer
multi-turn-consistency-scorer.description: Grade whether a twin stayed consistent across a multi-turn conversation

soul-writing-analyzer.name: Soul Writing Analyzer
soul-writing-analyzer.description: Extract communication patterns from writing samples

twin-trait-extractor.name: Twin Trait Extractor
twin-trait-extractor.description: Extract quantitative personality traits from twin documents

twin-confidence-analyzer.name: Twin Confidence Analyzer
twin-confidence-analyzer.description: Analyze completeness and confidence of twin personality definition

twin-import-analyzer.name: Twin Import Analyzer
twin-import-analyzer.description: Extract personality insights from imported external data

twin-spoken-written-compare.name: Twin Spoken-vs-Written Comparison
twin-spoken-written-compare.description: Compare a person's spoken transcript against their writing samples and surface communication-style differences

twin-image-identity-analyze.name: Twin Image Identity Analyzer
twin-image-identity-analyze.description: Analyze a photo of a person and extract visible appearance and self-presentation descriptors for their digital twin

pipeline-idea-expansion.name: Pipeline — Idea / Beat Sheet
pipeline-idea-expansion.description: Turn a rough seed idea for one issue or episode into a tight beat sheet with logline, theme, beats, setting, and any new characters. Spine for prose, comic script, TV script, and storyboard stages.

pipeline-prose.name: Pipeline — Prose Draft
pipeline-prose.description: Draft an 800–1500 word short-story version of one issue/episode from the beat sheet. Includes H2 scene breaks so downstream comic and TV adaptations have stable anchor points.

pipeline-comic-script.name: Pipeline — Comic Book Script
pipeline-comic-script.description: Adapt the prose draft into a Marvel/DC house-format comic-book issue script (page → panel → description / caption / dialogue / SFX). One of two parallel script stages, both downstream of prose.

pipeline-teleplay.name: Pipeline — TV Teleplay
pipeline-teleplay.description: Adapt the prose draft into a single-episode TV teleplay with sluglines, present-tense action, dialogue, and act breaks. One of two parallel script stages, both downstream of prose.

pipeline-editorial-analysis.name: Pipeline — Editorial Reader-Emotion Analysis
pipeline-editorial-analysis.description: Read one issue's reader-facing content (prose, comic script, or teleplay) and map the reader's emotional journey section by section, plus plot tension, character arcs, and protagonist detection. Feeds the series Editorial Roadmap. Runs at `heavy` tier — analyzes full issue content.

pipeline-reverse-outline.name: Pipeline — Reverse Outline (scene segmentation + plotline tagging)
pipeline-reverse-outline.description: Read the whole drafted manuscript and produce a reverse outline: identify the recurring plotlines, then segment the prose into scenes (each tagged to the plotline it advances, with POV, setting, and narrative/action/dialogue mix). Feeds the color-coded Reverse Outline view and is the canonical scene-segmentation substrate other editorial checks read. Runs at `heavy` tier — reads the full stitched manuscript.

pipeline-continuity-bible.name: Pipeline — Continuity Bible (established-facts ledger)
pipeline-continuity-bible.description: Read the whole drafted manuscript and extract concrete, checkable facts about the story world — physical traits, ages, dates/elapsed time, locations, possessions, world rules, and who-knows-what-when. Auto-seeded from universe canon (locked canon = ground truth) and learned from prose; feeds the browsable Series Continuity view and is the substrate the canon-contradiction / timeline / knowledge-leak checks read. Runs at `heavy` tier — reads the full stitched manuscript.

pipeline-extract-scenes.name: Pipeline — Extract Scenes from TV Teleplay
pipeline-extract-scenes.description: Parse an already-formatted TV teleplay into a structured scenes[] list (one entry per slugline) the storyboards stage can render. Defers to character / setting bibles for canonical names and slugline reuse.

pipeline-audio-cues.name: Pipeline — Audio Cue Planner
pipeline-audio-cues.description: Break an episode's narrative beats into a small ordered list of per-arc-beat musical cues (one cue per emotional movement, not per scene), each with a self-contained text-to-music prompt + human label. Drives the whole-episode 'generated' audio mode; timing is placed at stitch time from rendered clip durations.

story-builder-idea-expand.name: Story Builder — Idea Expand
story-builder-idea-expand.description: Expand a one-line seed idea into a working title, logline, and a world-and-arc-ready premise for the Unified Story Builder's first step (seed intake mode). Seeds the universe starter prompt and the series premise.

story-builder-reader-map.name: Story Builder — Reader Map
story-builder-reader-map.description: Plan the reader/viewer's experience across a series arc — hooks, payoffs, emotional beats, and cliffhangers — built on the protagonist arc and Vonnegut shape. Distinct from the protagonist arc; lives at series.arc.readerMap.

story-builder-reader-map-refine.name: Story Builder — Reader Map Refine
story-builder-reader-map-refine.description: Refine an existing reader map against free-text feedback, returning the full revised map plus a changes list and rationale (the AI-feedback affordance for the Story Builder reader-map step).

story-builder-arc-refine.name: Story Builder — Plot Arc Refine
story-builder-arc-refine.description: Refine an existing plot arc's narrative fields (logline, summary, protagonist arc, themes) against free-text feedback, returning the full revised arc plus a changes list and rationale (the AI-feedback affordance for the Story Builder plot-arc step). Does not re-plan the season breakdown.

pipeline-arc-overview.name: Pipeline — Series Arc Overview
pipeline-arc-overview.description: Plan the top-level multi-season story spine for a series from its bible: a series-level logline, summary, themes, protagonist arc, and a 2–5 season breakdown with per-season loglines, ending hooks, and episode-count targets. Runs at `heavy` tier — most expensive single call in the pipeline.

pipeline-season-episodes.name: Pipeline — Season Episode Breakdown
pipeline-season-episodes.description: Plan the episode-by-episode breakdown for one season of a multi-season series. Slots N episodes between the season's pickup state and ending hook, respecting prior seasons' continuity, the protagonist's whole-series arc, and a balanced mix of pilot / complication / midpoint / b-plot / all-is-lost / finale arc roles.

pipeline-arc-verify.name: Pipeline — Arc Verification
pipeline-arc-verify.description: Cross-season continuity pass over an authored arc: catches character contradictions, dropped subplots, episode-count vs arc-weight mismatch, unresolved finale hooks, arc-role imbalance, and theme drift. Returns actionable { severity, location, problem, suggestion } issues — empty list means clean.

pipeline-volume-verify.name: Pipeline — Volume / Season Verification
pipeline-volume-verify.description: Deep continuity pass on a single volume / season: volume-internal arc shape, within-volume continuity, beat-level escalation (issues with beats), promise drift, boundary continuity with neighbor volumes, cast economy, volume-scope world-entity drift, and obvious length-vs-weight mismatches. Complement to the cross-volume arc verify, deeper because the scope is smaller. Returns actionable { severity, location, problem, suggestion } issues — empty list means clean.

pipeline-beat-continuity.name: Pipeline — Whole-Manuscript Beat Continuity
pipeline-beat-continuity.description: Whole-book beat-level continuity pass over every issue's beat sheet — the altitude between cross-volume synopsis verify and the full scripts. Catches dropped cliffhangers/unresolved setups, finale drift from the arc's intended ending, promised through-lines that never land, duplicated 'firsts', volume-to-volume hand-off breaks, and cross-issue character-state contradictions BEFORE the expensive full-script stage. Returns actionable { severity, location, problem, suggestion } issues — empty list means the beats cohere.

pipeline-beat-continuity-resolve.name: Pipeline — Beat Continuity Auto-Resolve
pipeline-beat-continuity-resolve.description: Auto-resolve whole-manuscript beat-continuity findings by rewriting the BEATS of the specific issues that caused each finding (idea.output) — the beat altitude, not the arc. Returns a sparse episodes[] of full corrected beat sheets keyed by series-global episodeNumber (+ seasonNumber), plus optional notes for anything needing an arc-level restructure.

pipeline-script-verify.name: Pipeline — Comic Script Verification
pipeline-script-verify.description: Craft pass over ONE issue's comic script before art: catches un-renderable panels, missing/malformed page-panel structure, panel-to-panel flow breaks, dialogue/art imbalance, within-issue continuity, and page-turn/beat placement. Used by Series Autopilot's per-issue script gate (advisory — surfaces findings, doesn't auto-rewrite). Returns actionable { severity, location, problem, suggestion } issues — empty list means sound.

pipeline-manuscript-completeness.name: Pipeline — Manuscript Completeness
pipeline-manuscript-completeness.description: Developmental editor pass over the ACTUAL drafted manuscript (comic script / prose / teleplay across all issues), not synopses. Surfaces what's missing to FINISH a near-complete draft: missing-content (beats/pages/scenes), arc-gap (themes/protagonist turns the draft doesn't yet deliver), character-gap (under-developed cast), pacing, and continuity. Returns categorized { severity, category, issueNumber, anchorQuote, location, problem, suggestion } findings — issueNumber + anchorQuote anchor each finding to a manuscript spot so the editor can jump to it; empty list means complete.

pipeline-manuscript-fix.name: Pipeline — Manuscript Fix
pipeline-manuscript-fix.description: Generate a surgical, anchored edit that closes one 'Finish the draft' editorial gap in a single issue's drafted text. Given the issue's manuscript + the finding (category/severity/problem/suggestion/anchorQuote), returns a { find, replace } JSON pair where `find` is a verbatim excerpt of the manuscript to locate and `replace` is that excerpt rewritten to close the gap — preserving the author's voice. The user can edit `replace` before accepting it into the manuscript.

pipeline-volume-cover-concepts.name: Pipeline — Volume Cover Concepts
pipeline-volume-cover-concepts.description: Generate FRONT and BACK cover-art concepts for one volume (season) of a comic series. Returns a {coverConcept, backCoverConcept} JSON pair — pure scene descriptions for the image-gen renderer (no masthead, logo, or typography). Lightweight per-season call; may seed the cover and/or backCover script slots when they're blank, and never clobbers a user edit.

pipeline-comic-cover-concepts.name: Pipeline — Comic Issue Cover Concepts
pipeline-comic-cover-concepts.description: Generate FRONT and BACK cover-art concepts for a single comic issue. Returns a {coverConcept, backCoverConcept} JSON pair — pure scene descriptions for the image-gen renderer (no masthead, issue-number tag, or typography). Lightweight per-issue call; may seed the cover and/or backCover script slots (gated by the request `target`) when they're blank, and never clobbers a user edit.

pipeline-series-title-logo.name: Pipeline — Series Title / Logo Design
pipeline-series-title-logo.description: Design the series masthead / logo typography from the series name + logline + style notes + universe influences. Returns a {titleLogo} JSON object — a single prose paragraph describing typography treatment, finish, color hints, and motifs to inject into cover prompts and TV title screens. Runs once on series creation from a universe; user can regenerate from the bible sidebar.

pipeline-series-generate.name: Pipeline — Generate Series Concept
pipeline-series-generate.description: Invent a new series within a universe — returns a {name, logline, premise, shape} JSON concept that tells a different story from existing series in the same universe, using the universe premise / style / influences / canon as seed material. Drives the 'Generate with AI' button on the New Series form; the concept pre-fills the form for the user to edit before creating.

pipeline-comic-panel-image-prompt.name: Pipeline — Comic Panel Image Prompt
pipeline-comic-panel-image-prompt.description: Refine a single comic panel's description into a richer image-gen-ready prompt. Uses caption / dialogue / sfx context + neighboring-panel continuity to elaborate without re-imagining the scene.

pipeline-storyboard-image-prompt.name: Pipeline — Storyboard Scene Image Prompt
pipeline-storyboard-image-prompt.description: Refine a single storyboard scene's description into a richer image-gen-ready prompt. Uses slugline + neighboring-scene continuity to elaborate without contradicting location or time of day.

pipeline-comic-page-refine-render.name: Pipeline — Comic Page Refine Render
pipeline-comic-page-refine-render.description: Apply a small author correction to an already-rendered comic page's render prompt for an image-to-image re-render — changes only what the instruction asks and preserves the rest of the page (no redraw from the comic script).

universe-character-expand.name: Universe — Expand Character Profile
universe-character-expand.description: Flesh out blank fields on one universe canon character — novelist depth (motivations / likes / mannerisms / relationships) plus graphic-novelist visual fields (silhouette / palette / expressions / props / hand gestures). Preserves populated fields and stays distinct from peer characters.

importer-classify.name: Importer — Content-Type Classification
importer-classify.description: Classify the first chunk of a source as short-story / novel / screenplay / comic-script. Runs at `light` tier — only sees ~4K chars from the head of the source, costs are negligible compared to the canon/arc/issue extractors.

importer-canon-extract.name: Importer — Universe Canon Extraction
importer-canon-extract.description: Reverse-engineer characters / places / objects from a finished short story / novel / screenplay / comic script. Runs at `heavy` tier — source can be book-length.

importer-arc-extract.name: Importer — Series Arc Extraction
importer-arc-extract.description: Extract series-level arc (logline / summary / themes / protagonistArc / shape) plus 1–5 seasons from a finished work. Persists into `series.arc` + `series.seasons[]`.

importer-issue-proposal.name: Importer — Issue Proposal
importer-issue-proposal.description: Split a finished work into PortOS pipeline issues with titles + arcPosition + arcRole + verbatim prose excerpts. Honors source-marked issue / chapter / volume headers.

pipeline-canon-describe-from-prose.name: Pipeline — Describe Nouns From Prose
pipeline-canon-describe-from-prose.description: Backfill descriptions for canon nouns (characters/places/objects) that have none, using ONLY what the manuscript prose establishes. Grades each noun prose support (sufficient/thin/none) so nouns the prose never describes surface as a manuscript-quality red flag instead of being invented.

pipeline-editorial-info-dumping.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Info-dumping
pipeline-editorial-info-dumping.description: Editorial check (#1284): flags info-dumping / 'as you know, Bob' exposition in the drafted manuscript — backstory bricks, maid-and-butler dialogue, front-loaded world rules — and suggests how to dramatize or trim each instead. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-interiority.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Protagonist interiority
pipeline-editorial-interiority.description: Editorial check (#1294): flags POV scenes that move a viewpoint character through events without developing their interiority across four dimensions — mind, objective, emotional response, and decision-making. Reads the stitched manuscript, infers POV when untagged, and names the missing dimension per finding. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-chekhov.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Chekhov's guns (setups & payoffs)
pipeline-editorial-chekhov.description: Editorial check (#1299): flags planted elements that never pay off (weapon, clue, secret, stated fear, promise, threat, skill introduced then dropped) and payoffs that arrive with no setup. Reads the stitched manuscript across chunks with a cross-chunk setup digest, and reconciles detected setups/payoffs against the authored reader-map hooks/payoffs. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-comic-page-turn.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Comic page-turn beats
pipeline-editorial-comic-page-turn.description: Editorial check (#1314): flags comic reveals/cliffhangers placed where the reader sees them early — on a page that shares its two-page spread with earlier pages rather than the first page after a page turn. Reads each issue's parsed comic-page layout and reconciles placement against the authored reader-map reveals/cliffhangers. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-dead-metaphor.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Dead / mixed metaphor & overwriting
pipeline-editorial-dead-metaphor.description: Editorial check (#1308): the judgment layer for tired language the deterministic cliché scan can't catch — dead or mixed metaphors, lightly-reworded novel clichés, and overwrought / purple prose. Reads the stitched manuscript; severity is advisory; skips knowing in-voice clichés. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-opening-start.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Weak opening (wrong start)
pipeline-editorial-opening-start.description: Editorial check (#1300): flags clichéd or mis-placed openings — "he wakes up" / alarm-clock / dream starts, weather and scene-setting preambles, and scenes that begin before the interesting moment. Reads the stitched manuscript, focusing on the story opening and each chapter/issue opening. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-mirror-description.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Mirror self-description
pipeline-editorial-mirror-description.description: Editorial check (#1300): flags the tired device of a character looking at themselves in a mirror or reflection so the prose can catalog their appearance, while leaving reflections that do real story work alone. Reads the stitched manuscript. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-dialogue-pleasantries.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Empty greeting / small-talk openings
pipeline-editorial-dialogue-pleasantries.description: Editorial check (#1300): flags scenes that open on empty pleasantries — greeting rituals and logistical small talk that carry no tension or information — while leaving pleasantries laced with subtext alone. Reads the stitched manuscript. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-kill-your-darlings.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Kill your darlings
pipeline-editorial-kill-your-darlings.description: Editorial check (#1300): surfaces precious, self-indulgent passages — showpiece descriptions, digressions, over-explaining, and favorite lines that stop the scene's momentum — as candidates to cut, judged by story need rather than quality. Reads the stitched manuscript. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-endings-cliffhanger.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Chapter-ending cliffhangers
pipeline-editorial-endings-cliffhanger.description: Editorial check (#1298): flags chapter/issue endings that resolve and settle ("soft landings") instead of ending on an unresolved beat that pulls the reader forward, leaving a clearly terminal final chapter alone. Reads the stitched manuscript and reconciles detected endings against the authored reader-map cliffhangers. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-sensory-balance.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Sensory balance
pipeline-editorial-sensory-balance.description: Editorial check (#1309): flags scenes that lean almost entirely on sight while sound, smell, touch, and taste are neglected, and sensory-bare scenes with almost no concrete grounding. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene segmentation (degrading to a whole-issue scan when absent), naming the missing sense per finding. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-white-room.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: White-room / ungrounded scene
pipeline-editorial-white-room.description: Editorial check (#1309): flags "white-room" scenes — dialogue and action in an undescribed void with no setting, blocking, or spatial grounding. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene segmentation (using each scene's recorded setting as a candidate signal, degrading to a whole-issue scan when absent). Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-interiority-balance.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Interiority balance (description vs. POV reaction)
pipeline-editorial-interiority-balance.description: Editorial check (#1623): flags visually dense but emotionally empty scenes — prose heavy on setting, blocking, and physical detail yet light on the viewpoint character's reaction, emotion, or thought, so description swamps interiority. Judges the description-to-interiority ratio within each scene. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene segmentation (degrading to a whole-issue scan when absent). Distinct from sensory.balance (the senses) and interiority.protagonist (whole-issue interiority absence). Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-arc-transitions.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Character-arc transitions
pipeline-editorial-arc-transitions.description: Editorial check (#1293): scans each character's scenes for genuine change moments (decision, realization, point-of-no-return, relapse, sacrifice) and proposes transition beats with anchor quotes. Reconciles detected change moments against the authored per-character arcs (series.characterArcs) and flags flat arcs — a carrying character with no transition scenes. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene map (degrading to a whole-issue scan when absent). Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-timeline-contradictio.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Timeline / canon contradiction
pipeline-editorial-timeline-contradictio.description: Editorial check (#1581): scans for internal contradictions against canon and chronology — a character who dies and reappears alive without explanation, an age contradiction (the bible says 16, the prose says 'in her 30s'), or an impossible timeline. Reconciles the stitched manuscript against the established canon character facts, the reverse-outline scene ordering, and the authored per-character arcs; degrades to a prose-only scan when no canon or outline exists. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-fact-accuracy.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Research / fact accuracy
pipeline-editorial-fact-accuracy.description: Editorial check (#1588): scans grounded fiction for contradictions to real-world facts the author has documented — a city placed in the wrong country, an anachronistic event or technology, a physiologically impossible feat. Reconciles the stitched manuscript against the author-supplied fact reference (series.factReference); distinct from the internal timeline/canon-contradiction check. Opt-in and gated on the per-series fact-critical flag so it never fires on pure fantasy. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-character-consistency.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Character consistency (unearned personality shift)
pipeline-editorial-character-consistency.description: Editorial check (#1582): flags unearned characterization changes — a reserved character who suddenly cracks jokes with no arc beat, an established trait (fear, allergy, skill) silently contradicted, or POV-character knowledge that changes mid-scene without on-page learning. Reconciles the stitched manuscript against the established canon character traits, the reverse-outline scene ordering, and the authored per-character arcs so an intentional, earned transition is NOT flagged; degrades to a prose-only scan when no canon or outline exists. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-object-motivation.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Unmotivated object interaction
pipeline-editorial-object-motivation.description: Editorial check (#1288): flags moments where a character treats an object as significant without earned motivation — no recorded attachment and no on-page setup. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the canon object↔character attachments. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-object-backstory.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Attachment backstory consistency
pipeline-editorial-object-backstory.description: Editorial check (#1288): flags object↔character attachments whose origin story contradicts the attached character's established background — conflicting people, places, eras, or timelines. Canon-only (no manuscript). Returns canon-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-style-conformance.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Style-guide conformance
pipeline-editorial-style-conformance.description: Editorial check (#1303): flags prose that drifts from the series style guide — wrong narrative tense, point-of-view person drift, or content/profanity beyond the configured rating ceiling. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the declared style-guide expectations. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-pov-rewrite.name: Pipeline — Perspective Rewrite (alternate POV)
pipeline-pov-rewrite.description: Rewrite one issue's drafted passage (prose, comic script, or teleplay) from a different cast character's point of view — the same events re-lensed through another character's interiority, wants, and blind spots. A non-destructive revision exercise; the canonical draft is untouched. Runs at `heavy` tier — re-renders the full passage.

pipeline-pov-analysis.name: Pipeline — Perspective Rewrite Analysis (what we learn)
pipeline-pov-analysis.description: Compare an original passage with its alternate-POV rewrite and report what the exercise reveals: new interiority surfaced, information the original POV was hiding, whether the new POV has a stronger claim to the scene, and concrete fold-back edits. Runs at `heavy` tier — reads both passages.

pipeline-editorial-telling-emotion.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Telling-not-showing emotion
pipeline-editorial-telling-emotion.description: Editorial check (#1306): the judgment layer for named-emotion statements ("she was sad", "he felt nervous") worth dramatizing through action/sensation/subtext. Reads the stitched manuscript; severity is advisory; skips dialogue, deliberate pacing, and already-shown emotion. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-on-the-nose.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: On-the-nose / subtext-free dialogue
pipeline-editorial-on-the-nose.description: Editorial check (#1307): the judgment layer for dialogue that states feeling/meaning outright with no subtext and "maid-and-butler" exchanges where characters tell each other what they both already know. Reads the stitched manuscript; severity is advisory; skips earned directness, functional exchanges, and dialogue whose subtext already works. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-voice-distinctiveness.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Character voice distinctiveness
pipeline-editorial-voice-distinctiveness.description: Editorial check (#1307): samples each character dialogue and flags interchangeable voices (everyone sounds like one narrator) plus drafted lines that contradict the canon speechPattern/speechAccent. Reads the stitched manuscript + authored voice profiles; rolls a per-character voice-sample digest across chunks. Returns issue-anchored findings naming differentiating tics.

pipeline-editorial-voice-consistency.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Narrative voice / tone consistency
pipeline-editorial-voice-consistency.description: Editorial check (#1586): the NARRATOR-voice sibling of voice-distinctiveness (which covers per-character dialogue). Fingerprints each issue's narrative tone (diction, register, humor, emotional temperature) and flags an unexplained tonal shift across issues (witty issue 1 → grim issue 3 → witty issue 5 = whiplash) plus drift from the style guide's intended voice. Does not flag a purposeful tonal modulation the story earns. Reads the stitched manuscript + the style guide's intended tone; rolls a per-issue tone fingerprint across chunks. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-plot-structure.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Plot structure & momentum
pipeline-editorial-plot-structure.description: Editorial check (#1310): the macro pathologies editors flag at the manuscript/arc level — a passive protagonist, deus ex machina / convenient coincidence, idiot plot, flat or unclear stakes that never escalate, a sagging middle with no try-fail rhythm, and dropped subplots. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene map + plotline coverage (reconciling fizzled threads against tagged plotlines) and the authored reader-map hooks/payoffs; degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no outline exists. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-head-hopping.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Head-hopping / POV discipline
pipeline-editorial-head-hopping.description: Editorial check (#1311): in a limited-POV scene, flags narration that enters another character's head, reports knowledge/perception the POV character couldn't have, or switches POV mid-scene without a break. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline POV-per-scene map (degrading to a whole-issue scan when absent) and the series style guide's POV person; no-ops for third-person omniscient. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-climax-agency.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Climax / resolution power (passive protagonist at the climax)
pipeline-editorial-climax-agency.description: Editorial check (#1583): judges whether the CLIMAX is the protagonist's hardest, most active choice (not a passive climax where an ally rescues them, the antagonist self-destructs, or a coincidence resolves the conflict) AND whether it resolves the story's core problem and lands the emotional/thematic argument (not just the surface plot). Reconciles the prose against the authored reader-map payoffs (series.arc.readerMap) and the declared themes (series.arc.themes), using the reverse-outline scene map to locate the climax; gates its verdict on the final manuscript part and degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no reader-map, themes, or outline exists. Complements plot.structure-momentum. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-reaction-proportional.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Emotional beat proportionality (reactions vs event magnitude)
pipeline-editorial-reaction-proportional.description: Editorial check (#1584): whether each character's emotional reactions are PROPORTIONATE to the magnitude of the events that befall them. Flags an under-reaction (a high-magnitude event — trauma, death, betrayal, major loss/win — that draws no on-page reaction and is never processed in later issues) and an over-reaction (a minor setback triggering grief/rage/despair out of all proportion). Uses the reverse-outline scene map to weigh event magnitude and attribute findings to the right issue, and carries unprocessed high-magnitude events across manuscript parts so a later part can flag a trauma introduced early and left unaddressed; degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no outline exists. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-secondary-arc.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Secondary-character arcs (recurring non-POV cast)
pipeline-editorial-secondary-arc.description: Editorial check (#1585): the non-POV sibling of pov.justified (#1295). Tallies recurring NON-POV characters from the reverse-outline scene map (present across multiple scenes but never holding the viewpoint) and judges whether each shows meaningful change — flagging a flat side character (same at the end as the start) or one who regresses with no purpose. Does not flag a genuine walk-on or a deliberately-static foil whose constancy is the point. Because a flat arc is a whole-story claim, the verdict lands on the final manuscript part and each recurring secondary's established state is carried across parts; degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no outline exists. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-theme-coherence.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Theme coherence / thematic throughline
pipeline-editorial-theme-coherence.description: Editorial check (#1317): whether the manuscript actually DELIVERS its declared themes (series.arc.themes), not just states them. Builds a per-theme setup/complication/payoff coverage map, flags a theme that is stated but never dramatized or dropped after the opening, detects a strong EMERGENT theme not in the arc, and checks that the climax lands the thematic argument. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the authored arc themes and the reverse-outline scene map; degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no outline or no themes exist. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-unmodeled-names.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Unmodeled proper nouns used as character names
pipeline-editorial-unmodeled-names.description: Editorial check (#1412): the LLM-assisted half of roster economy (#1292). Surfaces capitalized proper nouns used as apparent CHARACTER names that are ABSENT from the story bible (canon.characters names + aliases), and classifies each (named person vs. place / organization / brand / honorific the deterministic roster.economy scan can't distinguish). Flags throwaway one-appearance unmodeled names, suggesting adding them to canon or leaving them unnamed. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the canon roster; treats an empty bible as every named proper noun being a candidate. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-eyeline-match.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Storyboard eyeline match
pipeline-editorial-eyeline-match.description: Editorial check (#1466): LLM scan of a scene's storyboard shot list for eyeline-match breaks — two characters in conversation whose gaze directions don't reciprocate across the cut, or a described eyeline that contradicts the shot's tagged screen direction. The judgment sibling of the deterministic visual.shot-continuity check; reads the per-issue storyboard shots (storyboard.shots). Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-appearance-continuity.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Storyboard appearance / prop continuity
pipeline-editorial-appearance-continuity.description: Editorial check (#1467): LLM diff of a scene's storyboard shot descriptions for appearance/prop continuity breaks — the same character described with conflicting wardrobe/hair/state, a prop that appears/vanishes/transforms, or a setting whose weather/time/layout contradicts across shots, with no action justifying the change. The semantic sibling of the deterministic visual.shot-continuity check (the shot parser matches characters by name but never diffs their descriptions); reads the per-issue storyboard shots (storyboard.shots). Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-comic-prose-sync.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Comic ↔ prose synchronization (hybrid issues)
pipeline-editorial-comic-prose-sync.description: Editorial check (#1589): cross-media consistency for hybrid comic+prose issues. Pairs each issue's prose narration with its authoritative comic pages (description + dialogue + caption + SFX) and flags SUBSTANTIVE divergences — a plot beat the prose narrates that no panel shows, panel dialogue that contradicts the prose (different words or a different speaker), or a chronology disagreement across the two media. Tuned to ignore ordinary medium-translation compression/reordering; reads the per-issue manuscript prose + comicScript.pacing. Runs one model call per hybrid issue. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-pacing-escalation-cur.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Pacing / intensity escalation curve
pipeline-editorial-pacing-escalation-cur.description: Editorial check (#1618): scores each issue's dramatic intensity and flags a series-wide escalation curve that fails to build — flat intensity (issue 1 reads as intensely as issue N), a front-loaded climax (the biggest beat lands early), or stakes that plateau or de-escalate across the arc. Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene map and a deterministic per-issue conflict-marker density tally (a grounding hint confirmed against the prose); degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no outline exists. Returns issue-anchored findings.

pipeline-editorial-arc-regression.name: Pipeline — Editorial Check: Character-arc regression / premature closure
pipeline-editorial-arc-regression.description: Editorial check (#1619): judges the SHAPE of each character's arc across the whole series and flags an unmotivated regression (growth then a purposeless revert, distinct from a dramatized relapse), a circular arc (ends where it began), or premature closure (the arc resolves early then goes flat for the rest of the series). Reads the stitched manuscript plus the reverse-outline scene map and the authored per-character arcs (series.characterArcs); degrades to a whole-manuscript scan when no outline or authored arcs exist. Whole-arc verdicts are gated to the final manuscript part. Returns character-anchored findings.
